judgements.reason: May 5 NVD-confirmed SGLang CVE-2026-5760 fix closes the immediate unpatched window, but the supply-chain GGUF vector + ~7,000 exposed MCP servers (half unauthenticated) backdrop preserves the AI-Infra KEV sub-catalog forcing function. Validation row 2026-05-05 (rel=3 dim=because) explicitly notes the supply-chain risk persists post-patch.
Latest bridge text correctly reframes the SGLang patch as 'closes the longest-running 2026 LLM-tooling RCE' while keeping the broader catalog need alive. No retracted or superseded narrative.
CISA + OWASP-track actor needs for sub-catalog publication and SBOM tagging on inference servers + agentic-CI runtimes target Q3 2026; no delivery yet.

New chain edges 2026-05-05 (downstream 5094f95c OWASP MCP Top 10 substrate, e4612827 LLM-tooling+kernel chain; upstream 140440d FOMC weak co-occurrence at 0.25, 082b0a07 GGUF SSTI, e4612827 IR post-mortem) form a coherent inference-runtime governance arc.
